texto: The greatest use of life is to spend it for something that will outlast it.
explicacao: Citação de Willam James sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The greatest use of life is to spend it for something that will outlast it.” articula motivacao, motivational-inspirational, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e termino.

frase_original: The greatest use of life is to spend it for something that will outlast it.

texto: This easy acceptance of an opaque limit to our speculative insight; this satisfaction with a Being whose character we simply apprehend without comprehending anything more about him, and with whom after a certain point our dealings can be only of a volitional and emotional sort; above all, this sitting down contended with a blank unmediated dualism - are they not the very picture of unfaithfulness to the rights and duties of our theoretic reasons?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Willam James que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “This easy acceptance of an opaque limit to our speculative insight; this satisfaction with a Bein…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, acceptance, opaque, com foco em termino e reflexao.
